'''Richard Moore''' (born 2 February 1981) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]].<ref name="Statistics at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Moore was born in [[Keighley]], [[West Yorkshire]], England

==Playing career==
He previously played for the [[Bradford Bulls]], [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]], [[Leigh Centurions]], [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders RL]], [[Leeds Rhinos]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]].

Moore originally started his career in the Leeds Rhinos Academy but never made a first team appearance. He then played for [[Bradford Bulls]], [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] and [[Leigh Centurions]],<ref>{{cite web|title=Moore makes switch to Centurions|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/4150007.stm|website=BBC Sport|access-date=9 November 2014|date=5 January 2005}}</ref> before joining [[Wakefield Trinity]] ahead of the 2007 season.<ref>{{cite web|title=Wildcats bring in Moore and Bibey|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wakefield/5393796.stm|website=BBC Sport|access-date=9 November 2014|date=29 September 2006}}</ref> Moore missed the start of the 2009 season after being diagnosed with [[Crohn's disease]].<ref>{{cite web|last1=Walker|first1=Gareth|title=Wakefield's Moore out for long spell with Crohn's disease|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2009/feb/11/wakefield-wildcats-richard-moore-crohns-disease|website=The Guardian|accessdate=9 November 2014|date=11 February 2009}}</ref> He left Wakefield at the end of the 2010 season to join [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.walesonline.co.uk/sport/rugby/rugby-news/rugby-league-crusaders-sign-prop-1882303|title=Rugby League: Crusaders sign prop Richard Moore|work=WalesOnline|date=27 December 2010|accessdate=9 November 2014}}</ref>

On 12 August 2011, Moore re-signed for Leeds, the club where he began his career as an academy player, but failed to make a first team appearance. He then had a second spell at Wakefield Trinity Wildcats in the 2014 season.
